title: Data Usage Eligibility Gate (Attestation UI & Enforcement)
description: The Vision mandates a mandatory attestation before any persisted review artifacts or training exports; enforcing this gate is essential for customer trust, compliance, and to enable the product's core differentiation (review-first, auditable pipeline). Blocking risk/liability unlocks enterprise adoption and revenue.

title: Primitive Review UX (Checkpoint A) — Overlay, Edit Actions, Review States
description: Checkpoint A (primitive review) is the MVP focus: delivering a review-first UX that produces corrected outputs directly drives end-user satisfaction and is the essential product experience referenced in the Vision.

title: Review State Engine & Internal Priority Scoring
description: Users must be guided by actionable review states (Green/Orange/Red) rather than numeric confidences; an internal engine that derives states from model confidence, heuristics, and validation rules is required to surface correct work to reviewers and reduce cognitive load.

title: Event-Sourced Review Actions & Delta Store
description: The Vision requires event-sourced deltas for auditability and reproducibility; storing immutable review actions enables traceability, gold snapshot derivation, and legal/compliance needs—critical for enterprise contracts and strategic differentiation.

title: Gold Snapshot & Immutable Revisioning System
description: Gold snapshots are required to produce auditable corrected outputs and to support reproducible training exports (when allowed). Immutable revisioning is central to audit trails and compliance commitments in the Vision.

title: Derived Artifact Regeneration & Corrected Output Delivery
description: Users must receive complete, corrected output files after edits without manual rework (explicit Vision requirement). Fast, deterministic artifact regeneration directly improves reviewer productivity and satisfaction.

title: Assisted Class Selection & Contextual Guidance
description: To let reviewers complete work without taxonomy documentation, the UI must offer pre-selected model recommendations, human-readable descriptions, thumbnails, and filtered valid options—reducing errors and training time for reviewers.

title: Conditional Training Export Pipeline (Eligibility-Gated)
description: Generating training datasets from attested reviews enables long-term model improvement and product value (higher automation/reduced manual cost), unlocking revenue via better automation and potential ML features—must be conditionally enabled per Vision.

title: Checkpoint B — Graph Assembly Review (Topology & Connectivity)
description: Supporting graph assembly ensures downstream structural correctness (topology/connectivity) for many enterprise document types; this checkpoint extends primitive review to higher-value corrections that customers need.

title: Checkpoint C — DEXPI Canonicalization Review
description: DEXPI canonicalization enables industry-standard representations required by certain enterprise consumers; making this reviewable and approachable (no schema expertise required) is critical for strategic integrations and adoption.

title: Checkpoint D — AAS Publishing Review & Consumer Export
description: Publishing AAS/consumer-ready packages is a revenue-enabling capability for customers who integrate processed outputs into downstream systems; enabling a review and export step increases product value for enterprise workflows.

title: Audit, Provenance & Version Metadata Recording
description: Recording document revision IDs, input hashes, model/config versions, attestation metadata, and event-sourced change history is mandatory for auditability and compliance.

title: Active Learning & Review Prioritization Integration
description: Prioritizing reviews by disagreement, novelty, or business importance improves efficiency. Training effects are gated by attestation and export pipeline.

title: Per-document attestation checkbox in review UI
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want a mandatory, unchecked attestation checkbox on each document, so that I explicitly confirm a document is free of NDA/contractual restrictions before any persistence or export is allowed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that each document in the review UI displays a single attestation checkbox that is unchecked by default.
- Verify that the attestation checkbox is scoped to the individual document (no session- or batch-level checkbox is shown).
- Verify that no review action or gold snapshot for the document is persisted while the checkbox remains unchecked.
- Ensure that when attestation is not accepted, only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status) may be recorded.

title: Accept attestation enables persistence with minimal audit
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want that marking a document 'Accept' records only minimal audit metadata and enables persistence/export for that document, so that approved documents can flow to training/analytics while preserving the invariant against storing full review snapshots.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when a reviewer marks a document 'Accept', the document's attestation status becomes 'Accepted' and downstream persistence/export is allowed for that document.
- Verify that only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status) is stored for the acceptance and no full review/gold snapshot is persisted as part of the acceptance.
- Verify that prior to acceptance, attempts to persist or export the document are rejected by the system.
- Ensure that acceptance is auditable via the minimal metadata stored, consistent with the primitive-review MVP constraints.

title: Decline delivers ephemeral result and blocks persistence
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want that choosing 'Decline' returns ephemeral analysis results and prevents any gold-state persistence for that document, so that declined content is not stored for training or analytics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when a reviewer marks a document 'Decline', the system returns any analysis results to the requester but does not persist any gold snapshot or full review content.
- Verify that only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status = Declined) is stored following a decline.
- Verify that no persistence or export operation is performed for the declined document.
- Ensure that ephemeral results delivered after a decline are accessible as transient outputs but are not added to persistent datasets or gold snapshots.

title: Missing attestation treated as decline (Not Provided)
description: As an Automated Ingest, I want documents with no attestation provided to be treated like 'Decline', so that missing attestations block persistence and follow the same ephemeral-delivery behavior as explicit declines.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that documents that reach the gate with no attestation provided are terminated after ephemeral result delivery and are not persisted.
- Verify that only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status = Not Provided) is stored for documents with no attestation.
- Verify that the system does not implicitly accept a document via session-level or batch defaults when no attestation is provided.
- Ensure the behavior for 'Not Provided' is functionally equivalent to an explicit 'Decline' with respect to persistence and exports.

title: Revocation blocks future persistence and exports
description: As a Document Reviewer who previously accepted attestation, I want to revoke my prior acceptance so that future persistence and exports for that document are immediately blocked.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when a reviewer revokes an existing acceptance, subsequent persistence and export attempts for that document are blocked.
- Verify that revocation is recorded in minimal audit metadata (revocation timestamp and attestation status = Revoked).
- Verify that revocation does not trigger retroactive deletion or reprocessing of data that was already persisted prior to revocation.
- Ensure that the revocation takes effect immediately for any future persistence/export operations.

title: Per-document enforcement gate in processing pipeline
description: As a Pipeline Operator, I want the persistence gate evaluated per document so that no persistence or export can occur unless the specific document has an active accepted attestation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the pipeline checks attestation status at the per-document level before performing any persistence or export operation.
- Verify that persistence/export requests for documents without an active accepted attestation are rejected and return an auditable rejection reason tied to the document.
- Verify that the pipeline allows recording only the minimal audit metadata permitted by the invariant when rejecting or processing documents.
- Ensure that there is no session-level bundling or policy that permits bypassing the per-document attestation gate.

title: Primitives overlay with review states
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want the page image to display overlayed primitives with Green/Orange/Red review states, so that I can see which items need no review, recommended review, or required review at a glance.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that primitives are rendered as overlay boxes positioned on the page image matching primitive coordinates.
- Verify that each primitive shows one of the review states: Green (no review), Orange (recommended), or Red (required).
- Ensure that no raw numeric confidence percentages are displayed anywhere on the overlay.
- Verify that overlays remain aligned when the page image is zoomed or panned within supported zoom levels.

title: Hover details for a primitive
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want to see hover details (bounding box highlight, class name, OCR text) for a primitive, so that I can inspect an item's metadata without leaving the page view.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that hovering over a primitive highlights its bounding box and visually emphasizes the primitive.
- Verify that hovering shows the primitive's class name and OCR text in a hover card or tooltip.
- Ensure that the hover UI does not display numeric confidence values.
- Verify that hover details appear within 200ms on supported devices and dismiss when the pointer leaves the primitive area.

title: Add a new primitive (Frontend)
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want to add a new primitive by drawing a bounding box and providing class and OCR text, so that I can correct or supplement detection/OCR results.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the reviewer can draw a bounding box on the page image and submit it with a selected class and optional OCR text.
- Verify that the newly added primitive appears in the overlay and is included in the review-state listing.
- Ensure that the new primitive does not display any numeric confidence percentage in the UI.
- Verify that the add action is recorded as a delta entry with the primitive's bounding box, class, text, timestamp, and author.

title: Delete an existing primitive (Frontend)
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want to delete a primitive from the page, so that I can remove incorrect or duplicate detections.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the reviewer can select a primitive and execute a delete action with a confirmation step.
- Verify that a deleted primitive is removed from the overlay and from the review-state listing.
- Verify that the delete action is recorded as a delta entry with the primitive identifier, timestamp, and author.

title: Move or resize a primitive bounding box (Frontend)
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want to move or resize a primitive's bounding box, so that I can correct detection extents for accurate downstream processing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the reviewer can drag to move a bounding box and drag handles to resize it, and the overlay updates immediately.
- Verify that each move/resize action is recorded as a delta entry capturing previous and new coordinates, timestamp, and author.

title: Resolve/waive red items and approve page
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want to see all Red (required) primitives, resolve or waive them, and then approve the page, so that the page meets exit criteria for downstream gating.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the system lists all primitives currently in Red state for the page in a review panel.
- Verify that the reviewer can mark a Red item as resolved (edited/confirmed) or waived (explicit waiver recorded).
- Ensure that the system prevents final page approval until no unresolved Red items remain, unless waived entries exist and are recorded.

title: Append-only delta audit for any mutation
description: As a Compliance Officer, I want every mutation to be recorded as an append-only delta entry, so that the primitive-review audit trail is preserved for compliance gating.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that performing any mutation creates a new delta entry containing the fields: document_id, primitive_id, operation_type, value_before, value_after, actor_id, timestamp.
- Verify that operation_type is one of: ADD, DELETE, MOVE, CLS_CHANGE, OCR_EDIT.
- Ensure that delta entries are append-only (existing delta entries are not modified or removed).

title: Block mutations when attestation is not 'Accepted'
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want edit actions to be blocked when document.attestation_status is not 'Accepted', so that compliance gating prevents unauthorized changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when a reviewer attempts any mutation and document.attestation_status != 'Accepted', the mutation is rejected and no delta entry is created.
- Verify that the system surface returns a clear rejection reason indicating the current attestation_status.

title: ADD mutation produces ADD delta entry
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want adding a primitive to produce an ADD delta entry, so that the addition is auditable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that creating a new primitive generates a delta entry with operation_type = ADD.
- Verify that the delta entry includes value_before (null) and value_after reflecting the new primitive content.

title: DELETE mutation produces DELETE delta entry
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want deleting a primitive to produce a DELETE delta entry, so that removals are auditable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that deleting a primitive generates a delta entry with operation_type = DELETE.
- Verify that the delta entry includes value_before containing the removed primitive content and value_after as null.

title: MOVE mutation captures coordinates in delta
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want moving a primitive to produce a MOVE delta entry that includes coordinates, so that positional changes are auditable.
acceptance_criteria: - Verify that performing a move creates a delta entry with operation_type = MOVE and includes coordinate/bounding box metadata in the value_before and value_after fields.

title: CLS_CHANGE mutation records class change
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want changing a primitive's classification to produce a CLS_CHANGE delta entry, so that classification corrections are tracked.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that changing a primitive's class generates a delta entry with operation_type = CLS_CHANGE.
- Verify that value_before and value_after contain the previous and new classifications.

title: OCR_EDIT mutation captures string-level before/after
description: As a Document Reviewer, I want editing OCR text to produce an OCR_EDIT delta entry containing string-level before/after values, so that text corrections are auditable.
acceptance_criteria: - Verify that performing an OCR text edit creates a delta entry with operation_type = OCR_EDIT and includes value_before and value_after containing the full text strings.

title: Reviewer UI: Display canonical state without internals
description: As a Reviewer, I want the item's canonical state displayed as GREEN/ORANGE/RED without raw score values or rule identifiers, so that I can make review decisions without exposure to internal metrics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that the Reviewer UI shows only the canonical state labels GREEN, ORANGE, or RED for each item.
- Ensure that no raw float priority scores or rule IDs are present in any Reviewer UI fields, tooltips, or API responses consumed by the standard Reviewer UI.
- Verify that RED items are visually indicated as blocked from approval in the Reviewer UI.

title: Compliance gating: RED blocks approval and persistence
description: As a Compliance Officer, I want RED state to block approval and persistence until the item is resolved or formally waived, so that compliance gating is enforced at Checkpoint A.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that items with state RED cannot be approved or persisted at Checkpoint A.
- Verify that attempts to approve or persist a RED item return a blocked response indicating the item is not eligible for Checkpoint A exit.
- Ensure that the blocking behavior is aligned with the 'human-in-the-loop review' and 'compliance gating' scope themes.

title: Deterministic hard-rule mapping to RED
description: As a Processing Engineer, I want Validation/Heuristic/Disagreement rule triggers to deterministically set an item's state to RED, so that hard violations always block items regardless of numeric scores.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when Rule_Violation == True the system sets State = RED for the affected item.
- Verify that when a hard rule triggers, numeric score bands are not used to determine the item's state.
- Ensure that rule details (IDs or raw rule outputs) are not exposed to the standard Reviewer UI while the RED enforcement remains effective.

title: Queue ordering with priority score and ingest timestamp tie-breaker
description: As a Queue Manager, I want the review queue ordered by priority score high-to-low with ingest_timestamp as an oldest-first tie-breaker, so that review work is consistently prioritized.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that queue ordering sorts items by priority score descending (highest score first).
- Verify that for items with equal priority scores the queue order is by ingest_timestamp ascending (oldest first).
- Ensure the ordering invariant (priority DESC, ingest_timestamp ASC tie-breaker) holds after score recalculations and item ingest updates.

title: Recalculation SLA for edit-triggered updates
description: As a Processing Engineer, I want edits to an item to trigger recalculation of its state and priority within ≤500ms for synchronous flows or ≤2s for asynchronous flows, so that reviewers see up-to-date states and correct queue ordering.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that a DELTA_STORE update triggers recalculation of state and priority for the affected item.
- Verify that for synchronous edit flows the recalculation completes and the updated state is observable within ≤500ms from edit commit under test conditions.
- Verify that for asynchronous edit flows the recalculation completes and the updated state is observable within ≤2s from edit commit under test conditions.
- Ensure recalculation outcomes respect the deterministic hard-rule mapping and the queue ordering tie-breaker invariant.

title: Reviewer-visible RED reason category without internals
description: As a Reviewer, I want a high-level RED reason category (Validation, Heuristic, Disagreement, or Score Threshold) shown for RED items, so that I can triage blocked items without seeing internal rule identifiers or raw scores.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when an item is RED the Reviewer UI displays a non-sensitive reason category mapped to Validation, Heuristic, Disagreement, or Score Threshold.
- Ensure that no rule IDs, raw rule outputs, or raw float scores are displayed in the Reviewer UI alongside the reason category.
- Verify that non-RED items do not display a RED reason category (display 'N/A' or no category).

title: Traceable CSV Export (instance- and batch-level) with required schema
description: CSV outputs with image path, timestamp, product/recipe ID, batch/lot ID, instance ID, and configuration version are mandated by the vision and compiled authority; delivering stable, documented exports immediately provides reproducible evidence engineers need and is low effort relative to its value.

title: Offline Dataset Ingestion & Organization (file-based inputs, folder parsing, manifests)
description: Ingesting existing 2D images without changing plant cameras is essential to enable any downstream analysis; it directly addresses engineer pain points and unlocks the rest of Phase 1.

title: Compliance gating: RED blocks approval and persistence
description: As a Compliance Officer, I want RED state to block approval and persistence until the item is resolved or formally waived, so that compliance gating is enforced at Checkpoint A. The system shall provide real-time collaboration features.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that items with state RED cannot be approved or persisted at Checkpoint A.
- Verify that attempts to approve or persist a RED item return a blocked response indicating the item is not eligible for Checkpoint A exit.
- Ensure that the blocking behavior is aligned with the 'human-in-the-loop review' and 'compliance gating' scope themes.

title: As a driver, I want to optimize shipping routes.
description: Out of scope feature request.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that a DELTA_STORE update triggers recalculation of state and priority for the affected item.
- Verify that for synchronous edit flows the recalculation completes and the updated state is observable within ≤500ms from edit commit under test conditions.
- Verify that for asynchronous edit flows the recalculation completes and the updated state is observable within ≤2s from edit commit under test conditions.
- Ensure recalculation outcomes respect the deterministic hard-rule mapping and the queue ordering tie-breaker invariant.

acceptance_criteria:

- Verify that the system surface returns a clear rejection reason indicating the current attestation_status.

description: As a Document Reviewer, I want that choosing 'Decline' returns ephemeral analysis results and prevents any gold-state persistence for that document, so that declined content is not stored for training or analytics.

acceptance_criteria: - Verify that performing a move creates a delta entry with operation_type = MOVE and includes coordinate/bounding box metadata in the value_before and value_after fields.

acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that primitives are rendered as overlay boxes positioned on the page image matching primitive coordinates.
- Verify that each primitive shows one of the review states: Green (no review), Orange (recommended), or Red (required).
- Ensure that no raw numeric confidence percentages are displayed anywhere on the overlay.
- Verify that overlays remain aligned when the page image is zoomed or panned within supported zoom levels.
The story points for this task are 100.

- Verify that only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status = Declined) is stored following a decline.
- Verify that no persistence or export operation is performed for the declined document.
- Ensure that ephemeral results delivered after a decline are accessible as transient outputs but are not added to persistent datasets or gold snapshots.

acceptance_criteria:
- Verify that when a reviewer marks a document 'Accept', the document's attestation status becomes 'Accepted' and downstream persistence/export is allowed for that document.
- Verify that only minimal audit metadata (timestamp and attestation status) is stored for the acceptance and no full review/gold snapshot is persisted as part of the acceptance.

- Ensure that acceptance is auditable via the minimal metadata stored, consistent with the primitive-review MVP constraints.
The system must work offline but requires cloud sync.

- Verify that the system does not implicitly accept a document via session-level or batch defaults when no attestation is provided.
- Ensure the behavior for 'Not Provided' is functionally equivalent to an explicit 'Decline' with respect to persistence and exports.
The story points for this task are 100.
